revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989326441|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:足立山]][[lld:Mont Adachi]] {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Adachi | image = [[File:Mount Adachi-01.jpg|300px]] | image Caption = | Elevation = 597.8 m | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|33.93|N|130|55|1.46|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title|name=Mount Adachi|qq=}} | Location = {{JPN|qq=}}
{{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Kitakyushu|en|Kitakyushu|qid=Q188806|lt=Kitakyushu|1=Kitakyushu}} [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]] | Range = Kikuyama Range | Type = | First Ascent = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.859425|130.9170|qq=}}|caption=|width=300|qq=}} Location of Mount Adachi (red dot) }} '''Mount Adachi''' (足立山, Adachiyama or Adachisan) is a {{ill|Mountain|en|Mountain|qid=Q8502|lt=Mountain|1=Mountain}} in [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], {{ill|Kitakyushu|en|Kitakyushu|qid=Q188806|lt=Kitakyushu|1=Kitakyushu}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, with an elevation of 597.8 m. It is also known by the name "Kirigatake" (霧が岳). Due to its higher altitude, the summit area experiences lower temperatures compared to the plains, and it is possible to see snowfall from the plains at least once a year. == Overview == Mount Adachi is located at the base of the [[Kiku Peninsula]] and forms part of the Kikuyama mountain range along with Mount Myōken (512 m) and Mount Tonoue (518.1 m). It is included in the [[:en:Kitakyushu Quasi-National Park|Kitakyushu Quasi-National Park]] and is one of the {{ill|Kyushu 100 Famous Mountains|ja|九州百名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu 100 Famous Mountains|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu 100 Famous Mountains|1=100 Famous Kyūshū Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370318}}. Its proximity to Kokura's urban center makes it a popular spot for enjoying [[night views]]. At the end of the southwestern ridge lies Mount Hōdai (442 m), where anti-aircraft gun positions were established during the [[wartime period]]. Stone structures from that time remain in the area. === Name Origin === The name "Mount Adachi" is said to originate from a legend during the {{ill|769|en|769|qid=Q30697|lt=769|1=769}} [[Usa Hachiman Oracle Incident]]. {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|qid=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}}, on his way to exile in {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|qid=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province|1=Ōsumi Province}}, injured his leg due to pursuit by emissaries of {{ill|Dōkyō|en|Dōkyō|qid=Q1272718|lt=Dōkyō|1=Dōkyō}}. His leg healed after bathing in the mountain's cold springs (considered sacred), and the mountain came to be called "Adachiyama" (meaning "leg stands"). A statue of Wake no Kiyomaro riding a [[:en:Boar|Boar]] stands within the grounds of the Adachiyama [[Myōken Shrine]] (Myōken-gū) or [[Oso Shrine]] located at the mountain's base in the Myōkenchō area of [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Fukuju-ji (Kitakyushu)|en|Fukuju-ji (Kitakyushu)|qid=Q124692981|lt=Fukujū-ji Temple (Kitakyushu)|1=Fukuju-ji (Kitakyushu)}} – A [[Bodaiji Temple|family temple]] of the {{ill|Kokura Domain|en|Kokura Domain|qid=Q1140943|lt=Kokura Domain|1=Kokura Domain}}'s ruling {{ill|Ogasawara family|ja|小笠原家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara family|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara family|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=小笠原氏|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=小笠原氏|1=Ogasawara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2975340}}, located at the foot of the mountain. * {{ill|Abeyama Park|ja|安部山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abeyama Park|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abeyama Park|1=Abeyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11451607}} – A park at the southern base of the mountain. * {{ill|Kokura Racecourse|en|Kokura Racecourse|qid=Q6426899|lt=Kokura Racecourse|1=Kokura Racecourse}} – Home to the special race "Adachiyama Tokubetsu." * {{ill|Galaxy Express 999|en|Galaxy Express 999|qid=Q92576|lt=Galaxy Express 999|1=Galaxy Express 999}} – [[:en:Reiji Matsumoto|Reiji Matsumoto]], a former resident of {{ill|Kokura|en|Kokura|qid=Q516373|lt=Kokura City|1=Kokura}}, mentioned that the scene of the "Galaxy Express 999 climbing into space was inspired by a steam locomotive ascending Mount Adachi" (mentioned in the promotional material for the {{ill|Kitakyushu Expo 2001|ja|北九州博覧祭2001|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyushu Expo 2001|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyushu Expo 2001|1=Kitakyushu Expo 2001|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400925}}). File:Mt.Adachi.jpg|View from Mount Hōdai File:Mount Adachi-02.JPG|View from Yokoshiru area in Kokuraminami Ward File:Kokura Jp night.jpg|Night view of Kokura City from Mount Adachi's slopes File:足立山妙見宮２.jpg|Adachiyama Myōken Shrine File:登山口の和気清麻呂像２.jpg|Statue of Wake no Kiyomaro riding a boar at the trailhead File:United Nations Memorial Cross.JPG|Memorial Cross (UN Monument) on Mount Adachi's slopes File:足立山山頂.jpg|Mount Adachi's summit == External Links == * [http://www.city.kitakyushu.lg.jp/kokurakita/file_0075.html Kitakyushu City Official Site – Adachi Park] * [http://www.city.kitakyushu.lg.jp/kokurakita/file_0084.html Kitakyushu City Official Site – Kiyomaro and Myōken Shrine in Kokurakita Ward] {{Kyushu 100 Famous Mountains|qq=}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|足立山|version=96782728|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:足立山]] {{Commons category|Mount Adachi}} [[lld:Mont Adachi]] {{wikidata link|Q11636998}} [[Category:小倉北区の地理]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]]
